techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Improves table, column, timestamp, filter, and join grounding for weak semantic slots.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Small token/runtime cost because retrieval is lexical and bounded.
techniques.generalization_impact: Uses schema roles and domain aliases instead of public-example templates.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Compact weak-SQL schema context with retrieved_tables, column_roles, value_links, timestamp_candidates, and join_candidates.
techniques.problem_solved: Weak models often choose semantically valid but wrong schema fields.
techniques.robustness_risk: Low, because outputs are advisory and still pass compiler, SQLValidator, SQL AST checks, and unit tests.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Prevents syntactically unsafe or schema-invalid SQL from executing.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Low incremental cost; existing validation is local.
techniques.generalization_impact: Applies to all generated SQL regardless of prompt family.
techniques.implementation_plan: Kept existing SQLValidator/sql_ast_tools validation in the weak compiler path.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Compiled SQL still goes through SQLValidator and SQL AST summary before execution.
techniques.problem_solved: Weak-model SQL plans can compile into malformed or unsafe SQL.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Improves intent-to-SQL shape by separating schema context, skeletons, and output structure.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Bounded context avoids large prompts; deterministic skeletons are compact.
techniques.generalization_impact: Teaches generic SQL shapes rather than memorizing public rows.
techniques.implementation_plan: Implemented generic skeleton retrieval and structured semantic-slot compilation.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Weak model emits semantic slots; compiler receives role-aware context and skeleton families.
techniques.problem_solved: Raw SQL prompting asks too much of weak models.
techniques.robustness_risk: Low, because skeletons contain no query ids, gold answers, or exact public SQL.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Reduces compound reasoning failures by decomposing prompt intent, domain, filters, relationships, SQL shape, and evidence grounding.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Moderate but bounded by one slot pass and one optional repair.
techniques.generalization_impact: Works at the semantic decomposition layer across paraphrases.
techniques.implementation_plan: Partially implemented through semantic slots plus enhanced compiler; deeper decomposition is a future candidate.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Weak-model flow already separates semantic slots, retrieval, compilation, validation, execution, and answer grounding.
techniques.problem_solved: Weak models conflate table choice, aggregation, filters, and final answer wording.
techniques.robustness_risk: Low while shadow-only; any future expansion needs token/runtime guardrails.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Rejects semantically wrong but valid SQL before execution.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Small local checks; no extra model call unless repair is enabled.
techniques.generalization_impact: Tests are role and ontology based, not public-query based.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Semantic unit tests check intent, table, column roles, filters, joins, aggregation, timestamp semantics, and overbroad queries.
techniques.problem_solved: Current weak scaffold has valid SQL with wrong semantics.
techniques.robustness_risk: Medium if too strict; mitigated by shadow-only use and repair hints.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Converts unit-test and validation errors into one bounded repair attempt.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Bounded cost; deterministic repair currently avoids extra model calls.
techniques.generalization_impact: Repairs general semantic slot errors such as wrong table, missing filter, and wrong timestamp role.
techniques.implementation_plan: Implemented as enhanced compiler repair_rounds=1 for weak SQL variants.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Repair operates on semantic slots/plans only; raw SQL is still never executed before validation.
techniques.problem_solved: One-shot weak slot compilation can fail to produce executable or semantically valid SQL.
techniques.robustness_risk: Low; repair cannot invent tables/columns outside SchemaIndex.

techniques.expected_correctness_impact: Improves weak model SQL-shape priors for count/list/status/date/relationship/detail prompts.
techniques.expected_efficiency_impact: Very low; only 1-3 small skeletons are selected.
techniques.generalization_impact: Skeletons are schema-role based and contain no public/dev answers.
techniques.maps_to_dashagent: Weak SQL candidates carry generic skeleton metadata for observability and future prompt use.
techniques.problem_solved: Weak models often choose the wrong aggregation or SQL shape.
